recipe.title: Mexican breakfast
recipe.description: Jamie Oliver's huevos rancheros recipe is like an epic combo of spicy tomato stew with runny eggs. Pile up into tortillas for an epic Mexican breakfast.

steps.descr: Peel and finely slice the onions and garlic
steps.instructions: Peel and finely slice the onions and garlic. Deseed and finely slice the peppers and chillies.

on_enter.say: Peel and finely slice the onions and garlic. Deseed and finely slice the peppers and chillies.

steps.descr: Get a large frying pan (make sure you’ve got a lid to go...
steps.instructions: Get a large frying pan (make sure you’ve got a lid to go with it) on a high heat and add several good lugs of olive oil.

on_enter.say: Get a large frying pan (make sure you’ve got a lid to go with it) on a high heat and add several good lugs of olive oil.

steps.descr: Add the onion, garlic, peppers, fresh and dried...
steps.instructions: Add the onion, garlic, peppers, fresh and dried chillies, bay leaves and a good pinch of sea salt and black pepper, and cook for 15 minutes, or until to softened and caramelised, stirring regularly.

on_enter.say: Add the onion, garlic, peppers, fresh and dried chillies, bay leaves and a good pinch of sea salt and black pepper, and cook for 15 minutes, or until to softened and caramelised, stirring regularly.

steps.descr: Pour in the tomatoes, using a spoon or potato masher to...
steps.instructions: Pour in the tomatoes, using a spoon or potato masher to break them up. Bring to the boil, then turn down to a medium heat and cook for a further 5 minutes to reduce the sauce.

on_enter.say: Pour in the tomatoes, using a spoon or potato masher to break them up. Bring to the boil, then turn down to a medium heat and cook for a further 5 minutes to reduce the sauce.

steps.descr: When you’ve got a nice thick tomato stew consistency,...
steps.instructions: When you’ve got a nice thick tomato stew consistency, have a taste and adjust the seasoning, if needed.

on_enter.say: When you’ve got a nice thick tomato stew consistency, have a taste and adjust the seasoning, if needed.

steps.descr: Slice the fresh tomatoes and lay them over the top of...
steps.instructions: Slice the fresh tomatoes and lay them over the top of the mixture.

on_enter.say: Slice the fresh tomatoes and lay them over the top of the mixture.

steps.descr: Use a spoon to make small wells in the tomato stew, then...
steps.instructions: Use a spoon to make small wells in the tomato stew, then crack in the eggs so they poach in the thick, delicious juices – try to crack them in as quickly as you can so they all get to cook for roughly the same amount of time.

on_enter.say: Use a spoon to make small wells in the tomato stew, then crack in the eggs so they poach in the thick, delicious juices – try to crack them in as quickly as you can so they all get to cook for roughly the same amount of time.

steps.descr: Season from a height, put the lid on and let the eggs...
steps.instructions: Season from a height, put the lid on and let the eggs cook for around 3 to 4 minutes, or until cooked to your liking.

on_enter.say: Season from a height, put the lid on and let the eggs cook for around 3 to 4 minutes, or until cooked to your liking.

steps.descr: Warm the tortillas while this is happening
steps.instructions: Warm the tortillas while this is happening. You can pop them into the oven at 180°C/350°F/gas 4 for a few minutes, microwave them for a few seconds or even lay them over the lid of the pan so they heat up as the eggs cook.

on_enter.say: Warm the tortillas while this is happening. You can pop them into the oven at 180°C/350°F/gas 4 for a few minutes, microwave them for a few seconds or even lay them over the lid of the pan so they heat up as the eggs cook.

steps.descr: Take the lid off and check your eggs by giving them a...
steps.instructions: Take the lid off and check your eggs by giving them a poke with your finger. When they’re done to your liking, turn the heat off and take the pan to the table with the warmed tortillas, the Cheddar and a grater so everyone can get involved and make their own. Personally, I like to grate a bit of cheese right on to a warm tortilla, spoon an egg and some of the wonderful tomato stew on top, wrap it up, and eat it right away. What a beautiful way to wake up!
